title: daemon/store: use_space_invite_code manual BEGIN IMMEDIATE pattern is fragile
description: daemon/src/store.rs:1163-1245: use_space_invite_code acquires a raw connection and issues 'BEGIN IMMEDIATE' manually rather than using pool.begin() (which issues BEGIN DEFERRED). The comment explains the reason correctly. However: the async block and manual COMMIT/ROLLBACK pattern is a footgun. If the inner async block panics (not just returns Err), the connection is dropped without an explicit ROLLBACK, leaving the transaction open until the connection is recycled by the pool. The pool recycles connections and SQLite should auto-rollback on close. But this relies on undocumented sqlx behavior. A future contributor modifying this code path might add a panic-able operation (e.g. unwrap) and silently leave transactions open. Consider using a scopeguard or Drop-based ROLLBACK, or document the panic behavior explicitly.
